libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Configure generated tests to run both JVM tests and native-image builds with `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED`, e.g. Gradle `Test.jvmArgs` and native-image `buildArgs`. For version `2.9.1`, avoid carrying over old `2.1.7` imports of internal classes such as `com.google.inject.internal.util.MapMaker` or `com.google.inject.internal.cglib.proxy.MethodProxy`; the resolved artifact contains the repackaged internal classes with `$`-prefixed names, while normal usage should prefer public `com.google.inject` APIs.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8858 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.sonatype.sisu:sisu-guice:2.9.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Existing `2.1.7` tests compile against internal class names that are absent in `2.9.1`, causing the reported javac failures.
Guice proxy/injection paths can fail at runtime on JDK 17+ with `InaccessibleObjectException` if `java.base/java.lang` is not opened.
Optional `slf4j-api` is declared by the artifact but is not required for core Guice coverage.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker service is needed, but meaningful Guice injector/proxy coverage on modern JDKs needs java.lang opened to unnamed modules.
